name: MacTutor
biography:
Ludwig Schlesinger started elementary school in Trnava and, moving to high school, he attended the Realschule in Presburg, now Bratislava ( Slovakia ) . He then studied mathematics and physics at the universities of Heidelberg and Berlin between 1896 and 1887 , and he received a doctorate from the University of Berlin in 1887 for a thesis on differential equations entitled: 횄혵ber lineare homogene Differentialgleichungen vierter Ordnung, zwischen deren Integralen homogene Relationen h횄쨋heren als ersten Grades bestehen 창혫혟 ( On linear homogeneous differential equations of the fourth order, between the integrals homogeneous relations higher than first degree ) . His thesis advisors were Lazarus Immanuel Fuchs and Leopold Kronecker .
In 1889 Schlesinger became an associate professor at the University of Berlin; in 1897 , invited professor at the University of Bonn, and in the same year he was appointed professor of mathematics at the University of Kolozsv횄징r, Hungary ( now Cluj, Romania ) . He served as head of the department of higher mathematics and, in 1906 - 07 , he was the dean of the Faculty of Mathematics and Sciences. In 1911 he left Kolozsv횄징r and moved to the University of Giessen, Germany, where he continued to teach until he retired in 1930 .
In 1902 Schlesinger was elected as a corresponding member of the Hungarian Academy of Sciences , and in 1909 he was honoured with the award of the Lobachevsky Prize.
Ludwig Schlesinger wrote many papers for scientific periodicals and journals. Papers such as Sur la d횄짤termination des fonctions alg횄짤briques uniformes sur une surface de Riemann donn횄짤e 창혫혟 ( On the determination of uniform algebraic functions on a given Riemann surface ) (1903) , 횄혵ber isoliertwertige Funktionen 창혫혟 ( On valent isolated functions ) (1905) , 횄혵ber asymptotische Darstellungen der L횄쨋sungen linearer Differentialsysteme als Funktionen eines Parameters 창혫혟 ( On asymptotic representations of the solutions of linear differential systems as functions of a parameter ) (1907) are examples of his publications in the frst decade of the 20 th century. Near the end of his career he published articles such as Parallelverschiebung und Kr횄쩌mmungstensor 창혫혟 ( Parallel shift and curvature ) (1928) , 횄혵ber die hypergeometrischen Differentialsysteme 창혫혟 ( On  hypergeometric differential systems ) (1928) , Neue Grundlagen f횄쩌r einen Infinitesimalkalkul der Matrizen 창혫혟 ( New bases for a infinitesimal calculus of matrices ) (1931) , and Weitere Beitr횄짚ge zum Infinitesimalkalkul der Matrizen 창혫혟 ( Related articles on infinitesimal calculus of matrices ) (1932) . Perhaps his most important work, as far as current mathematical research is concerned, was 횄혵ber eine Klasse von Differentialsystemen beliebiger Ordnung mit festen kritischen Punkten 창혫혟 ( On a class of differential systems of arbitrary order with fixed critical points ) which he published in Crelle's Journal in 1912 .  In this paper Schlesinger formulated the problem of isomonodromy deformations for a certain matrix Fuchsian equation. The problem which he was tackling was a special case of Hilbert 's 23 rd problem, namely:- Prove the existence of linear differential equations having a prescribed monodromic group. Schlesinger solved a particular case of the problem using Poincar횄짤 's theory of the Fuchsian zeta-functions. The paper introduces what today are known as the Schlesinger transformations and Schlesinger equations which have an important role in differential geometry.
Schlesinger is the author of Handbuch der Theorie der Linearen Differentialgleichungen 창혫혟 ( Handbook of the theory of linear differential equations ) ( B G Teubner, Leipzig; Vol 1 , 1895 ; Vol 2 Part 1 , 1897 ; Vol. II, Part 2 , 1898) . This was reprinted by the Johnson Reprint Corporation, New York-London, in 1968 . He also published Einf횄쩌hrung in die Theorie der gew횄쨋hnlichen Differentialgleichungen auf funktionentheoretischer Grundlage 창혫혟 ( Introduction to the theory of ordinary differential equations on a functional theoretical basis ) ( third edition, Leipzig, 1922) . In 1920 he published Raum, zeit und relativit횄짚tstheorie; gemeinverst횄짚ndliche vortr횄짚ge 창혫혟 ( Space, time and relativity; generally comprehensible lectures ) and four years later he publised the important monograph on automorphic functions Automorphe Funktionen 창혫혟 ( Automorphic functions ) ( W de Gruyter & Co, Berlin, 1924) . In 1926 Schlesinger published a book on Lebesgue integration and Fourier series in collaboration with Abraham Plessner . The work studies trigonometric series and the boundary behaviour of analytic functions.
Another of Schlesinger's interests was the history of mathematics and he made a number of important contributions to this topic. He translated Descartes ' Geometrie into German and this was published by Mayer and M횄쩌ller, Berlin, in 1894 . A second edition was published in 1923 and reprinted by Wissenschaftliche Buchgesellschaft, Darmstadt, 1969 . He was an admirer of Gauss and he wrote related essays, like 횄혵ber Gauss' Arbeiten zur Funktionentheorie 창혫혟 ( On Gauss's work on complex analysis ) 222 S Berlin, J Springer ( C F Gauss . Werke Bd. X, 2) . published in 1933 or C F Gauss: Fragmente zur Theorie des arithmetisch- geometrischen Mittels aus den Jahren 1797 - 1799 창혫혟 ( C F Gauss: Fragments on the theory of the arithmetic-geometric mean from the years 1797 to 1799 ) in G횄쨋ttinger Nachrichten published in 1912 .
After reading Zo횄징rd Ge횄쨋cze 's papers during his stay at the University of Kolozsv횄징r, Ludwig Schlesinger suggested to him that he write down his ideas and submit them to Comptes Rendus for publication.
At the Franz Joseph University he was one of the most dedicated organisers of the centenary festivities dedicated to the hundredth anniversary of J횄징nos Bolyai . He identified the house in which J횄징nos Bolyai was born and he held an excellent conference on the centenary festivity: Libellus post saeculum quam Ioannes Bolyai de Bolya anno MDCCCII a. d. XVIII kalendas Ianuarias Claudiopoli natus est ad celebrandam memoriam eius immortalem ex consilio ordinis mathematicorum et naturae scrutatorum Regiae LitterarumUniversitatis Hungaricae Francisco- Josephinae Claudiopolitanae editus [ red. Ludovicus Schlesinger ] .
He collected, in three volumes, the most important work of his advisor, Lazarus Fuchs , who was also his father-in-law. During his stay in Kolozsv횄징r ( Cluj ) , Schlesinger contributed significantly to the advancement of mathematics in the city. He, Gyula Farkas and Gyula V횄징lyi , had a decisive role in the establishment of an excellent mathematics library within the university.
